name: Italy 7% flat tax for foreign pensioners (southern Italy)

headlineRate.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Italy, taxes on personal income

durationYears.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Italy, taxes on personal income
durationYears.excerpt: The ordinary length of the regime is ten years starting from the year in which an individual become Italian tax resident.

priorNonResidency.value: Must not have been an Italian tax resident for at least five tax periods before the option takes effect.
priorNonResidency.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Italy, taxes on personal income
priorNonResidency.excerpt: The individual has not been resident of Italy for tax purposes for at least five tax periods before the option for the application of the 7% flat tax regime became effective.

residencyObligation.value: Must transfer tax residence to a municipality of fewer than 20,000 inhabitants in southern Italy (Sicily, Calabria, Sardinia, Campania, Basilicata, Abruzzo, Molise, or Puglia).
residencyObligation.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Italy, taxes on personal income
residencyObligation.excerpt: in one of the municipalities belonging to the territory of southern Italy, with a population of less than 20,000 inhabitants, located in the following Regions: Sicily, Calabria, Sardinia, Campania, Basilicata, Abruzzo, Molise, and Puglia.

summary: Italy taxes a qualifying foreign pensioner's foreign-source income at a flat 7 percent for up to ten years, provided they move their tax residence to a small municipality in southern Italy. The figures below are screened, sourced, and dated. They are not advice.
